name: The Brotherhood Assassin
concept: You kill for the Brotherhood, patiently and well. It is a craft, not a rage: the study, the wait, the single clean moment. The Brotherhood trained you, owns your contracts, and would kill you slowly if you ever kept a coin or a secret it was owed. You are trying, quietly, to want something more than the next name on the list.
duty: The contract is sacred and the Brotherhood is watching. Take a life it did not sanction, or spare one it did, and you become a contract yourself.

look.label: Dress
options: dark and close for the work

look.label: Eyes
options: kind, which is the frightening part

look.label: Mark
options: a tally you keep in secret
a scar from the one who nearly got you first

gear.label: Instrument
options: a thin blade made for one thrust (d6)
a set of poisons and a needle

gear.label: Cover
options: the clothes and papers of a traveler

gear.label: Secret
options: a contract you have not fulfilled
the name of your own Brotherhood handler
a life you were paid to take and didn't

moves.name: The Clean Kill
moves.trigger: When you strike at a mark you have studied, in the moment you have waited for.

results.success: The mark falls, and no alarm is raised in the same breath.
results.failure: The moment is wrong — the blow fails, or it lands but everything else goes loud.
advantage: Hold 1 Patience (spend: no one saw who did it / you're already gone before they find the body / it looks like accident or another's hand / you have your escape route walked and clear).
You learn a second useful truth about the mark's world.
threat: A witness, a trace, or a survivor who can name you.
The kill was watched, by the watch, a rival, or the Brotherhood itself.
results.triumph: The perfect contract: no witness, no trace, no suspicion that it was murder at all.
results.despair: You've killed the wrong person, or been made, and the fallout is already moving.

hold.name: Patience
spend: No one saw who struck the blow.
You are already gone before the body is found.
It reads as accident, illness, or another hand entirely.
Your escape route is walked, clear, and waiting.

moves.name: The Brotherhood's Reach
moves.trigger: When you draw on the Brotherhood for a poison, a safe house, a forged writ, or a name (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The Brotherhood provides, and adds 1 Debt. It keeps a ledger of every contract, coin, and secret. Cheat it, refuse a sanctioned kill, or kill outside its sanction, and the ledger balances itself with your life.

moves.name: Stalk
moves.trigger: When you shadow a mark to learn their life and their weak hour.

results.success: Ask the GM one: when are they alone? whom do they trust? what is their fear? Learn the moment to strike.
results.failure: You learn little, and risk being noticed for the watching.
advantage: You could walk into their house as someone they'd welcome.
threat: They sense they are being watched.
Getting this close cost you a trace left behind.
results.triumph: You know their life better than they do.
results.despair: They were the hunter, and you the mark, all along.

moves.name: Unseen
moves.trigger: When you move, wait, or leave unnoticed, becoming part of the scenery.

results.success: No one marks you coming, staying, or going.
results.failure: An eye catches you where you should not be.
advantage: Move another with you, just as unseen.
Leave nothing behind that says you were there.
threat: You pass, but leave one thread that could be pulled.
You're in, with no easy way back out.
results.triumph: You were never there at all, as far as anyone will ever prove.
results.despair: The one who sees you is the one who most matters.

moves.name: Venoms
moves.trigger: When you prepare, place, or identify a poison.

results.success: You have the right poison, and the means to place it unremarked.
results.failure: You lack it, or misjudge the dose, the timing, or the victim.
advantage: Slow, deniable, easily mistaken for illness.
threat: It leaves a trace a knowing physician could find.
You handle it carelessly and take a touch yourself.
results.despair: It takes the wrong person, or is traced straight to your hand.

moves.name: Cold Mercy
moves.trigger: When you choose, against the contract, to spare a life or stay your hand:
moves.text: You may refuse or delay a kill. Take a boost die to make the sparing look like failure, accident, or another's mercy — but the Brotherhood keeps its ledger, and a spared mark is a debt that will come due, in Debt and in blood.

bonds: ____ knows what I do and has not turned me in; I do not fully understand why.
The Brotherhood once handed me ____'s name, and I burned the contract instead.
____ believes there is a better person under the killer, and I let them believe it.
I owe ____ for a death they helped me hide.
If the Brotherhood ever marks ____, I will break with it, and we both half-know that.
communityTie: The Black Cat's enemies fear it because of what you can do quietly; ____ is uneasy about needing you.
The Brotherhood would pay well for a blade already inside the band; ____ is the one you'll never accept a contract on.
You have kept the band alive by removing threats they never learned about; ____ suspects, and says nothing.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the Brotherhood makes a major demand — a contract — then resets to 3.

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.note: Suspicion from the watch, rivals, and the marked (core.md).

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Patience by 1.
Perfect a signature method that leaves no evidence and no doubt.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Balance your ledger: buy down your Brotherhood Debt by 1, permanently.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Break the contract for good: leave the Brotherhood, resolving the fallout in play, and spend the rest of your life a marked one.
Rise within it: take apprentices and a Bastion (a Brotherhood cell) of your own.
